channel.title: AnantaOps Articles
channel.description: Engineering, security, and ops-intelligence articles from the AnantaOps team.

item.title: Your On-Call Team Is Drowning: The Alert Fatigue Numbers Nobody Wants to Publish
item.description: Most alerts aren't actionable, most on-call engineers know it, and most outages that follow were preceded by an ignored warning. This is the problem we started AnantaOps to fix.
item.author: Aarav Shah — Platform Engineering

item.title: We Read the Reports on AI Agents in Production. Here's Everything That Breaks.
item.description: Multi-agent AI systems fail quietly, not loudly. A checklist of the failure modes that actually show up once agents are handling real work.
item.author: Priya Nair — AI/ML

item.title: Prompt Injection Isn't a Research Paper Anymore — It's a Patch Note
item.description: The exact shape of system that reads your Slack, Jira, and Gmail is the exact shape of system prompt injection targets. Here's what that means in practice.
item.author: Meera Iyer — Security

item.title: The 19% Slowdown Nobody Wants to Talk About: AI Coding Tools, Honestly
item.description: Usage and revenue for AI coding tools are going vertical. The evidence on whether they actually make you faster is a lot messier — and worth understanding before you bet your roadmap on it.
item.author: Priya Nair — AI/ML

item.title: The Memory-Safety Deadline Already Passed: Where Rust and Go Actually Stand
item.description: Rust is in the Linux kernel's core language list now. CISA set a memory-safety roadmap deadline. Here's what actually changed versus what's still hype.
item.author: Aarav Shah — Platform Engineering

item.title: AI Didn't Fix Your DevOps Problems — It Just Made Them Louder
item.description: DORA's latest research calls it the 'mirror and multiplier' effect: AI accelerates teams that already have good process, and amplifies the dysfunction of teams that don't.
item.author: Daniel Osei — DevOps

item.title: Your Observability Stack in 2026: Prometheus 3, Grafana 13, and the Quiet eBPF Takeover
item.description: Prometheus, Grafana, OpenTelemetry, and Docker haven't stood still. Here's what's actually changed, and what to know before you pick your next infra project.
item.author: Daniel Osei — DevOps

item.title: The Cloud-Native Stack in 2026: What's Actually Winning
item.description: Kubernetes is basically default now. The interesting fights are one layer up — sidecar-less mesh, WASM's real niche, and what the October DNS outage actually taught us.
item.author: Daniel Osei — DevOps

item.title: Security Training Isn't Optional — Here's What Every Org Should Actually Teach
item.description: Annual compliance videos don't stop breaches. Here's the training that does, and why it has to be role-specific instead of one-size-fits-all.
item.author: Daniel Osei — DevOps

item.title: Secure Coding Practices That Actually Stick
item.description: Checklists get ignored. Here's what actually changes how a team writes code — tooling, review habits, and a few hard rules with no exceptions.
item.author: Meera Iyer — Security

item.title: Basic Security Principles Every Engineer Should Know
item.description: Least privilege, defense in depth, fail securely — the handful of ideas that prevent most of the incidents we actually see.
item.author: Meera Iyer — Security

item.title: IDOR, SSRF, and a Very Real Secrets Problem: OWASP in Practice, Not in Theory
item.description: Every classic OWASP bug class has a 2025-dated, named incident attached to it now. Here's what they actually looked like.
item.author: Meera Iyer — Security

item.title: Your Employees Are Already Using 14 AI Tools You Don't Know About
item.description: Shadow AI isn't an enterprise problem anymore. SMBs are the least protected, and it's showing up in exactly the tools ops teams already run.
item.author: Priya Nair — AI/ML
